# UC-12.1.2 · Branch Protection Bypasses

## Description

Direct pushes to protected branches bypass code review, introducing unreviewed code to production. Detection ensures process compliance.

## Value

Direct pushes to protected branches bypass code review, introducing unreviewed code to production. Detection ensures process compliance.

## Implementation

Ingest GitHub/GitLab audit logs. Alert on any push to protected branches (main, release) without PR merge. Alert on branch protection rule changes. Correlate with deployment events.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub audit log, GitLab API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: GitHub/GitLab audit log, push events to protected branches.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest GitHub/GitLab audit logs. Alert on any push to protected branches (main, release) without PR merge. Alert on branch protection rule changes. Correlate with deployment events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:audit" action="protected_branch.policy_override"
| table _time, actor, repo, branch, action
```

Understanding this SPL

**Branch Protection Bypasses** — Direct pushes to protected branches bypass code review, introducing unreviewed code to production. Detection ensures process compliance.

Documented **Data sources**: GitHub/GitLab audit log, push events to protected branches. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub audit log, GitLab API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Branch Protection Bypasses**): table _time, actor, repo, branch, action


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GitHub and GitLab audit or activity views for the same time range. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (bypass events), Timeline (protection violations), Single value (bypasses this month — target: 0).

## SPL

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:audit" action="protected_branch.policy_override"
| table _time, actor, repo, branch, action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.All_Changes
  by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.action span=1h
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (bypass events), Timeline (protection violations), Single value (bypasses this month — target: 0).
